Firebending.rules_text_template: Whenever this creature attacks, add {R} repeated X times. This mana lasts until end of combat.
tag_notes: Add mana_sink if the card has a meaningful way to spend the mana.
Do not tag as ritual unless your system intentionally treats repeatable conditional mana as ritual.
Firebending.example: Firebending 3 = Whenever this creature attacks, add {R}{R}{R}. This mana lasts until end of combat.

Waterbend.rules_text_template: While paying a waterbend cost, you can tap your artifacts and creatures to help. Each one pays for {1}.
Waterbend.mechanic_summary: Tap your own artifacts and creatures to reduce a special activated cost.
tag_notes: Add mana_sink if the waterbend ability is repeatable and meaningfully consumes extra mana.
Add card_draw, token_maker, or other functional tags based on what the waterbend ability actually does.

Earthbend.rules_text_template: Target land you control becomes a 0/0 creature with haste that's still a land. Put X +1/+1 counters on it. When it dies or is exiled, return it to the battlefield tapped.
Earthbend.mechanic_summary: Animates a land, grows it with +1/+1 counters, and gives it built-in resilience.
tag_notes: Add threat if the card meaningfully pressures the opponent.
Do not assume landfall; earthbend itself is not a land-entry trigger.
Earthbend.example: Earthbend 2 = Target land you control becomes a 0/0 creature with haste that's still a land. Put two +1/+1 counters on it. When it dies or is exiled, return it to the battlefield tapped.

Airbend.rules_text_template: Exile them. While each one is exiled, its owner may cast it for {2} rather than its mana cost.
Airbend.mechanic_summary: Temporary exile and recast from exile for a fixed alternate cost.
tag_notes: Add etb_payoff when the card rewards re-entering permanents or recasting them.
Add token_maker or other payoff tags from the rest of the card text.
Airbend.example: Airbend any number of other target nonland permanents you control.

Forage.rules_text_template: To forage, exile three cards from your graveyard or sacrifice a Food.
Forage.mechanic_summary: Alternative payment using graveyard resources or Food sacrifice.
tag_notes: Add sac_payoff, sac_fodder, food, or graveyard_payoff depending on the specific card.
Do not auto-tag sacrifice_outlet; forage is a payment option, not a repeatable sacrifice engine by itself.
Forage.example: To forage, exile three cards from your graveyard or sacrifice a Food.

Deathtouch.rules_text_template: Any amount of damage this deals to a creature is enough to destroy it.
Deathtouch.mechanic_summary: Makes even small amounts of damage lethal to creatures.
tag_notes: Usually not a primary tag by itself in your taxonomy unless combat dominance matters.
Can support fight cards or defensive combat roles.
Deathtouch.example: A creature with deathtouch destroys creatures it deals damage to.

Defender.mechanic_summary: Creature is restricted to blocking or noncombat utility roles.
tag_notes: Usually not enough for a tag on its own.
May matter later if you add a walls/defender-matter taxonomy.
Defender.example: A creature with defender can't attack.

Double Strike.rules_text_template: This creature deals both first-strike and regular combat damage.
Double Strike.mechanic_summary: Deals combat damage twice in combat.
tag_notes: Often supports voltron or combat-scaling plans.
Not always tag-worthy unless combat concentration is a real role.
Double Strike.example: A creature with double strike deals damage in both combat damage steps.

Enchant.rules_text_template: This Aura can enchant only [specified object or player].
Enchant.mechanic_summary: Restricts what an Aura can legally enchant.
tag_notes: Being an Aura alone should not automatically imply aura strategy support.
Use aura only if the card is actually aura-relevant in your taxonomy.

Equip.rules_text_template: Equip {cost} means {cost}: Attach this permanent to target creature you control. Activate only as a sorcery.
Equip.mechanic_summary: Moves Equipment onto creatures for repeatable attached bonuses.
tag_notes: Often also supports go_tall depending on the card.
Use equipment as the primary mechanical tag.

First Strike.rules_text_template: This creature deals combat damage before creatures without first strike or double strike.
First Strike.mechanic_summary: Gives combat initiative in the first combat damage step.
tag_notes: Usually not a standalone taxonomy tag.
May support combat-focused or go_tall cards incidentally.
First Strike.example: A creature with first strike deals combat damage earlier.

Flash.rules_text_template: You may cast this spell any time you could cast an instant.
tag_notes: Flash alone usually does not determine taxonomy role.
Can matter for protection, combat_trick, threat, or interaction depending on full card text.
Flash.example: A spell with flash can be cast at instant speed.

Flying.rules_text_template: This creature can't be blocked except by creatures with flying or reach.
tag_notes: Only use evasive_threat if the creature is a real offensive threat.
Small defensive flyers may not deserve the tag.
Flying.example: A creature with flying is hard to block without flying or reach.

Haste.rules_text_template: This creature can attack and {T} as soon as it comes under your control.
Haste.mechanic_summary: Allows immediate attacking and tap activation.
tag_notes: Do not auto-tag haste_enabler just because a card has haste.
Use haste_enabler when the card grants haste or strategically enables immediate use.
Haste.example: A creature with haste can attack the turn it enters.

Hexproof.rules_text_template: This permanent or player can't be the target of spells or abilities your opponents control.
tag_notes: Use protection when self-shielding is strategically meaningful.
Use protection_enabler when the card grants safety to others.
Hexproof.example: A creature with hexproof can't be targeted by opponents' spells or abilities.

Indestructible.rules_text_template: Effects that say 'destroy' don't destroy this. If its toughness is 0 or less, it's still put into its owner's graveyard.
Indestructible.mechanic_summary: Resists destruction effects and lethal damage.
tag_notes: Can also contribute to sticky_threat when attached to a real threat.
Not all indestructible cards should be tagged as threats.
Indestructible.example: An indestructible permanent can't be destroyed.

Intimidate.rules_text_template: This creature can't be blocked except by artifact creatures and creatures that share a color with it.
tag_notes: Deprecated in modern design, but mechanically still evasion.
Only use evasive_threat if the body is offensively meaningful.
Intimidate.example: A creature with intimidate is difficult to block outside shared-color or artifact creatures.

Landwalk.rules_text_template: This creature can't be blocked as long as defending player controls a land of the specified type.
Landwalk.mechanic_summary: Conditional unblockability based on land types.
tag_notes: Use only if the card is a meaningful pressure piece.
Landwalk is an evasion mechanic, not lands-matter.

Lifelink.rules_text_template: Damage dealt by this permanent also causes its controller to gain that much life.
tag_notes: Only use lifegain_enabler when the life gain is strategically meaningful.
Can also support life_drain-adjacent shells, but does not itself drain opponents.
Lifelink.example: A creature with lifelink gains its controller life when it deals damage.

Protection.rules_text_template: This permanent or player can't be damaged, enchanted/equipped, blocked, or targeted by [specified quality].
Protection.mechanic_summary: Grants broad shielding from a specified quality.
tag_notes: Can function as both defense and evasion.
Use protection as the primary tag unless your card is more clearly an evasion enabler or threat.

Reach.rules_text_template: This creature can block creatures with flying.
tag_notes: Usually defensive and not a standalone taxonomy tag.
Can matter if you later add blocker or defensive_body tags.
Reach.example: A creature with reach can block flyers.

Shroud.rules_text_template: This permanent or player can't be the target of spells or abilities.
Shroud.mechanic_summary: Prevents all targeting, including from its controller.
tag_notes: Older cousin of hexproof.
Can contribute to sticky_threat when attached to a real win condition.
Shroud.example: A creature with shroud can't be targeted by anyone.

Trample.rules_text_template: Excess combat damage can be assigned to the defending player, planeswalker, or battle.
Trample.mechanic_summary: Pushes excess combat damage through blockers.
tag_notes: Trample often matters most on large or scaling threats.
Do not use evasive_threat if the body is not actually pressuring.
Trample.example: A creature with trample can assign excess damage past blockers.

Vigilance.rules_text_template: Attacking doesn't cause this creature to tap.
Vigilance.mechanic_summary: Allows attacking without giving up blocking or tap-state utility.
tag_notes: Usually not a standalone taxonomy tag.
Can matter with tap_synergy, attack_trigger, or defensive combat roles depending on card context.
Vigilance.example: A creature with vigilance attacks without tapping.

Ward.rules_text_template: Whenever this permanent becomes the target of a spell or ability an opponent controls, counter it unless that player pays [cost].
Ward.mechanic_summary: Taxes opposing targeted interaction aimed at this permanent.
tag_notes: Use protection when the ward text is a meaningful part of the card's resilience.
Ward alone does not make a card a sticky_threat unless the card is also a real threat.

Banding.rules_text_template: This creature can attack or block in a band, letting its controller make certain combat damage assignment decisions.
Banding.mechanic_summary: Obscure combat-coordination ability that changes how creatures attack, block, and assign combat damage in groups.
tag_notes: Usually not a meaningful standalone tag in your current taxonomy.
If you later add deep combat coordination tags, banding may deserve special handling.
Banding.example: A creature with banding can attack in a band with other creatures.

Rampage.rules_text_template: Whenever this creature becomes blocked, it gets +N/+N until end of turn for each creature beyond the first blocking it.
Rampage.mechanic_summary: Punishes multiple blockers by scaling temporary combat stats.
tag_notes: Usually combat-focused and only tag-worthy if the card is meaningfully built around combat scaling.
Not the same as evasive_threat; it rewards being gang-blocked rather than bypassing blockers.

Cumulative Upkeep.rules_text_template: At the beginning of your upkeep, put an age counter on this permanent, then sacrifice it unless you pay [cost] for each age counter on it.
Cumulative Upkeep.mechanic_summary: Escalating upkeep payment that becomes harder to sustain over time.
tag_notes: Usually not a taxonomy tag by itself.
May matter indirectly for sacrifice, temporary value, or upkeep-scaling designs, but the card's actual function should drive tagging.

Flanking.rules_text_template: Whenever a creature without flanking blocks this creature, the blocking creature gets -1/-1 until end of turn.
Flanking.mechanic_summary: Combat-pressure ability that weakens blockers lacking flanking.
tag_notes: Use evasive_threat only if the card is a real pressure piece; flanking is not literal evasion but often functions similarly in combat.
Could be left untagged if your system wants to reserve evasive_threat for more direct evasion only.
Flanking.example: A creature with flanking shrinks nonflanking blockers.

Phasing.rules_text_template: During each untap step, before the active player untaps permanents, all phased-in permanents with phasing that player controls phase out, and all phased-out permanents that player controlled when they phased out phase in.
Phasing.mechanic_summary: Causes permanents to alternate between phased-in and phased-out states without changing zones.
tag_notes: Only use protection when phasing is being used meaningfully to dodge interaction or preserve permanents.
Do not confuse phasing with blink; phased-out permanents do not leave and return to the battlefield.
Phasing.example: A permanent with phasing periodically phases out and back in.

Buyback.rules_text_template: You may pay an additional [cost] as you cast this spell. If you do, put this card into your hand as it resolves.
Buyback.mechanic_summary: Optional extra payment that returns the spell to your hand after resolution for reuse.
tag_notes: Can also support spell_recursion if you want keyword-level priors to be more specific, but recursion is safer as a default.
Add mana_sink if the repeatable extra payment is a meaningful role of the card.

Cycling.rules_text_template: {cost}, Discard this card: Draw a card.
Cycling.mechanic_summary: Turns a card into card draw by discarding it.
tag_notes: Cycling is both draw and discard, so it synergizes with graveyard strategies.
Use discard_outlet only if discard is strategically relevant in the deck.

Echo.rules_text_template: At the beginning of your upkeep, if this came under your control since your last upkeep, sacrifice it unless you pay its echo cost.
Echo.mechanic_summary: Delayed additional cost or sacrifice after entering the battlefield.
tag_notes: Echo itself is not a payoff or engine.
Tag based on the ETB effect or sacrifice synergy of the card instead.

Kicker.rules_text_template: You may pay an additional {cost} as you cast this spell for an enhanced effect.
Kicker.mechanic_summary: Optional additional cost for upgraded spell effects.
tag_notes: Kicker often represents scalable value for extra mana.
Do not tag cost_reduction; kicker increases cost rather than reducing it.

Flashback.rules_text_template: You may cast this card from your graveyard for its flashback cost. Then exile it.
Flashback.mechanic_summary: Allows casting a spell from the graveyard for a second use.
tag_notes: Flashback is both recursion and alternate casting.
Prefer spell_recursion over generic recursion.

Madness.rules_text_template: If you discard this card, you may cast it for its madness cost instead of putting it into your graveyard.
Madness.mechanic_summary: Allows casting a card at a reduced or alternate cost when discarded.
tag_notes: Madness requires discard synergy to function properly.
Do not assume discard_outlet unless the card or deck supports discarding.

Morph.rules_text_template: You may cast this card face down as a 2/2 creature for {3}. Turn it face up any time for its morph cost.
Morph.mechanic_summary: Alternative hidden casting mode with later face-up conversion for value or surprise.
tag_notes: Morph itself is usually not a taxonomy tag.
Tag based on the face-up effect, ETB-adjacent value, combat trick use, or payoff text on the specific card.

Storm.rules_text_template: When you cast this spell, copy it for each spell cast before it this turn.
Storm.mechanic_summary: Burst spell-count payoff that scales with how many spells were cast earlier in the turn.
tag_notes: Prefer storm as the primary tag because the mechanic is specifically about burst spell count.
copy_spell is also appropriate because storm explicitly copies the spell.

Affinity.rules_text_template: This spell costs {1} less to cast for each [quality] you control.
Affinity.mechanic_summary: Cost reduction scaling with a board class such as artifacts.
tag_notes: Add artifacts_matter, enchantments_matter, or other class-specific synergy tags based on what affinity counts.
Do not tag ramp; affinity reduces spell cost rather than generating mana.

Entwine.rules_text_template: Choose both modes instead of one if you pay the entwine cost.
Entwine.mechanic_summary: Additional payment upgrades a modal spell to use all listed modes.
tag_notes: Entwine is usually a mana-sink style upgrade mechanic, not a standalone archetype tag.
Tag the spell's actual effects separately, such as card_draw, spot_removal, or token_maker.

Modular.rules_text_template: This permanent enters with N +1/+1 counters on it. When it dies, you may put its +1/+1 counters on target artifact creature.
Modular.mechanic_summary: Artifact creature counter mechanic that preserves and transfers +1/+1 counters on death.
tag_notes: Use counters_payoff when the card meaningfully benefits from counter scaling or transfer.
Death_payoff fits because modular creates value when the creature dies.
Not every modular card is automatically a payoff-heavy build-around, but these are strong defaults.

Sunburst.rules_text_template: This permanent enters with a +1/+1 counter on it for each color of mana spent to cast it, or this spell enters with charge counters that way if it's not a creature.
Sunburst.mechanic_summary: Scales counters based on color diversity spent to cast the spell.
tag_notes: Use mana_fixing on supporting cards, not on the sunburst card itself unless it also fixes mana.
Add counters_payoff if the card explicitly benefits from having or moving those counters beyond simply entering with them.

Bushido.rules_text_template: Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, it gets +N/+N until end of turn.
Bushido.mechanic_summary: Combat-scaling ability that boosts the creature when it engages in combat.
tag_notes: Usually supports concentrated combat on one creature.
Not a standalone archetype tag unless combat scaling is central to the card's role.

Soulshift.rules_text_template: When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, you may return target Spirit card with mana value N or less from your graveyard to your hand.
Soulshift.mechanic_summary: Death-triggered typal recursion for Spirit cards.
tag_notes: Prefer recursion rather than reanimation because the card returns to hand.
Typal is appropriate because the mechanic explicitly cares about Spirits.

Splice.rules_text_template: As you cast a [quality] spell, you may reveal this card from your hand and pay its splice cost. If you do, add this card's effects to that spell.
Splice.mechanic_summary: Adds a card's effects onto another spell while keeping the spliced card in hand.
tag_notes: Splice is unusual card-advantage-adjacent spell layering, but spellslinger is the safest default.
Do not auto-tag copy_spell; splice adds text, it does not copy a spell.

Offering.rules_text_template: You may cast this card any time you could cast an instant by sacrificing a [quality] and paying the difference in mana costs between this and the sacrificed permanent, including color requirements.
Offering.mechanic_summary: Instant-speed cost-cheat mechanic that uses a tribal sacrifice to help cast a larger spell.
tag_notes: Use typal because the sacrifice requirement names a creature type.
Sac_payoff is reasonable because sacrificing the appropriate permanent is explicitly rewarded with a rate advantage.

Ninjutsu.rules_text_template: {cost}, Return an unblocked attacker you control to hand: Put this card onto the battlefield from your hand tapped and attacking.
Ninjutsu.mechanic_summary: Combat-deployment ability that swaps an unblocked attacker for this creature from hand.
tag_notes: Cost_cheat fits because the creature bypasses normal casting.
Evasive_threat is only appropriate when the card is a real offensive pressure piece; many ninjas are.
Many ninjutsu cards also want combat_damage_trigger from their own text.

Epic.rules_text_template: For the rest of the game, you can't cast spells. At the beginning of each of your upkeeps, copy this spell except for its epic ability.
Epic.mechanic_summary: Locks out future spell casting in exchange for repeated copies of the same spell each upkeep.
tag_notes: Value_engine fits because epic creates ongoing repeated value over time.
Do not tag storm or spellslinger; epic shuts off future spell casting rather than rewarding continued spell chaining.

Convoke.rules_text_template: Your creatures can help cast this spell. Each creature you tap while casting this spell pays for {1} or one mana of that creature’s color.
Convoke.mechanic_summary: Uses creatures as a resource to reduce spell costs.
tag_notes: Very close to waterbend mechanically—good validation of your tap_synergy tag.
Do not tag ramp; this is cost reduction, not mana generation.

Dredge.rules_text_template: If you would draw a card, you may instead put exactly N cards from the top of your library into your graveyard. If you do, return this card from your graveyard to your hand.
Dredge.mechanic_summary: Replaces draws with self-mill to recur the card.
tag_notes: One of the strongest graveyard enablers in the game.
Prefer recursion over card_draw since it replaces drawing.

Transmute.rules_text_template: {cost}, Discard this card: Search your library for a card with the same mana value as this card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.
Transmute.mechanic_summary: Turns a card into a tutor for another card with matching mana value.
tag_notes: Very clean tutor mechanic.
Discard_outlet applies because discarding is part of the activation cost.

Bloodthirst.rules_text_template: If an opponent was dealt damage this turn, this creature enters the battlefield with N +1/+1 counters on it.
Bloodthirst.mechanic_summary: Rewards dealing damage before casting the creature.
tag_notes: Often part of aggressive strategies.
Do not tag counters_payoff unless the card scales further from counters.

Haunt.rules_text_template: When this spell resolves or the creature dies, exile it haunting target creature. When that creature dies, trigger the haunt effect again.
Haunt.mechanic_summary: Delayed death-trigger recursion through haunting another creature.
tag_notes: Acts like delayed recursion/value, not traditional recursion.
Often creates multi-stage death value.

Replicate.rules_text_template: When you cast this spell, copy it for each time you paid its replicate cost.
Replicate.mechanic_summary: Scales spell copies based on how much extra mana you pay.
tag_notes: Very similar to storm but controlled via mana instead of spell count.
Strong mana_sink usage.

Forecast.rules_text_template: During your upkeep, you may reveal this card from your hand and pay its forecast cost to produce an effect.
Forecast.mechanic_summary: Repeatable hand-based activated ability during upkeep.
tag_notes: Functions as a slow, repeatable value engine.
Tag additional effects (draw, tokens, etc.) separately.

Graft.rules_text_template: This creature enters with N +1/+1 counters on it. Whenever another creature enters, you may move a +1/+1 counter from this creature onto it.
Graft.mechanic_summary: Distributes +1/+1 counters to new creatures entering the battlefield.
tag_notes: Strong synergy with ETB strategies.
Counters_payoff fits because the card leverages counters across the board.

Recover.rules_text_template: When a creature is put into your graveyard from the battlefield, you may pay the recover cost. If you do, return this card from your graveyard to your hand. Otherwise, exile it.
Recover.mechanic_summary: Conditional recursion triggered by creature deaths.
tag_notes: Ties recursion to death events.
Prefer recursion over reanimation since it returns to hand.

Ripple.rules_text_template: When you cast this spell, you may reveal the top N cards of your library. You may cast any revealed cards with the same name without paying their mana costs.
Ripple.mechanic_summary: Chains additional copies of the same spell from the library.
tag_notes: Not technically copying spells, but casting additional ones.
Synergizes with decks built around repeated names.

Split Second.rules_text_template: As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.
Split Second.mechanic_summary: Prevents most interaction while the spell is on the stack.
tag_notes: Represents stack-level protection for the spell.
Can also be thought of as anti-interaction, but protection is closest in your system.

Suspend.rules_text_template: Rather than cast this card from your hand, you may pay {cost} and exile it with N time counters. At upkeep, remove one. When the last is removed, cast it without paying its mana cost.
Suspend.mechanic_summary: Delays casting a spell for a cheaper or alternate cost over time.
tag_notes: Cost_cheat fits because you often pay less than the original cost.
Cast_from_elsewhere applies since the spell is cast from exile.

Vanishing.rules_text_template: This permanent enters with N time counters. At upkeep, remove one. When the last is removed, sacrifice it.
Vanishing.mechanic_summary: Temporary permanent with a countdown lifespan.
tag_notes: Vanishing itself is not a payoff.
Tag based on ETB, death_payoff, or other effects on the card.

Absorb.rules_text_template: If a source would deal damage to this creature, prevent N of that damage.
Absorb.mechanic_summary: Reduces incoming damage to the creature.
tag_notes: Only tag protection if damage prevention is meaningful to the card’s role.
Often too minor to tag on its own.

Aura Swap.rules_text_template: {cost}: Exchange this Aura with an Aura card in your hand.
Aura Swap.mechanic_summary: Swaps an Aura on the battlefield with one from your hand.
tag_notes: Supports aura-based strategies.
Not card advantage by itself—just flexibility.

Delve.rules_text_template: Each card you exile from your graveyard while casting this spell pays for {1}.
Delve.mechanic_summary: Uses graveyard as a resource to reduce spell cost.
tag_notes: Strong synergy with self_mill.
Do not tag ramp; this is cost reduction.

Fortify.rules_text_template: {cost}: Attach this Fortification to target land you control. Activate only as a sorcery.
tag_notes: Very niche mechanic.
Tag based on what the Fortification actually does if relevant.

Frenzy.rules_text_template: Whenever this creature attacks and isn't blocked, it gets +N/+0 until end of turn.
Frenzy.mechanic_summary: Rewards attacking unblocked with bonus damage.
tag_notes: Depends on evasion to trigger, so often pairs with evasion strategies.
Only tag if the creature is a real offensive threat.

Gravestorm.rules_text_template: When you cast this spell, copy it for each permanent put into a graveyard this turn.
Gravestorm.mechanic_summary: Storm variant that scales with permanents dying.
tag_notes: Strong synergy with sacrifice and board wipes.
Storm is appropriate because it’s a scaling burst mechanic.

Poisonous.rules_text_template: Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, that player gets N poison counters.
Poisonous.mechanic_summary: Applies poison counters on combat damage.
tag_notes: Poison is a separate win condition, but your taxonomy does not yet include poison/infect archetypes.
Consider adding a future poison_payoff or alternate_wincon tag.

Transfigure.rules_text_template: {cost}, Sacrifice this permanent: Search your library for a creature card with the same mana value as this permanent and put it onto the battlefield. Activate only as a sorcery.
Transfigure.mechanic_summary: Turns a creature into another creature of equal mana value directly onto the battlefield.
tag_notes: Cost_cheat applies because the creature bypasses casting.
Sacrifice_outlet fits since the card sacrifices itself as part of its function.

Champion.rules_text_template: When this permanent enters the battlefield, sacrifice it unless you exile another [quality] you control. When this leaves the battlefield, return the exiled card.
Champion.mechanic_summary: Temporarily exiles another permanent you control and returns it when this leaves.
tag_notes: Acts like a delayed blink.
Typal applies because champion requires a specific creature type.

Changeling.rules_text_template: This card is every creature type.
Changeling.mechanic_summary: Counts as all creature types simultaneously.
tag_notes: Strong enabler for tribal/typal decks.
Not a payoff by itself.

Evoke.rules_text_template: You may cast this spell for its evoke cost. If you do, it's sacrificed when it enters the battlefield.
Evoke.mechanic_summary: Alternative cheaper cost for a one-shot ETB effect.
tag_notes: Often used for ETB value rather than as a creature.
Add death_payoff if the card benefits from being sacrificed.

Hideaway.rules_text_template: When this permanent enters, look at the top N cards of your library, exile one face down, then put the rest on the bottom. You may play the exiled card later if a condition is met.
Hideaway.mechanic_summary: Stores a card from the library to be cast later under certain conditions.
tag_notes: Selection plus delayed access.
Add payoff tags based on the condition.

Prowl.rules_text_template: You may cast this spell for its prowl cost if you dealt combat damage to a player with a [quality] this turn.
Prowl.mechanic_summary: Conditional cost reduction tied to dealing combat damage with a specific creature type.
tag_notes: Often tied to Rogues.
Encourages evasive damage strategies.

Reinforce.rules_text_template: {cost}, Discard this card: Put N +1/+1 counters on target creature.
Reinforce.mechanic_summary: Turns a card in hand into +1/+1 counters via discard.
tag_notes: Good bridge between counters and discard strategies.

Conspire.rules_text_template: As you cast this spell, you may tap two untapped creatures you control that share a color with it. If you do, copy it.
Conspire.mechanic_summary: Copies spells by tapping creatures as an additional cost.
tag_notes: Another strong validation of tap_synergy.
Similar to convoke but for copying instead of cost reduction.

Persist.rules_text_template: When this creature dies, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield with a -1/-1 counter on it.
Persist.mechanic_summary: Returns a creature to the battlefield once after it dies.
tag_notes: Functions like limited reanimation.
Sticky_threat applies if the creature is a real threat.
